The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Texas's 38th Congressional District, encompassing Republican-leaning Houston suburbs like Cypress, Tomball, and Greater Katy with a Cook PVI of R+10, drives trader consensus favoring the Republican Party at 83.5% implied probability, consistent with Solid Republican ratings from Cook Political Report and historical GOP victories by 25+ points in 2022 and 2024. Incumbent Wesley Hunt vacated the seat for a U.S. Senate bid, but the March 3 primaries solidified Democrat Melissa McDonough as nominee after her 51.6% win, while Republicans head to a May 26 runoff between Trump-endorsed Jon Bonck (47.7% in primary) and Shelly deZevallos. GOP fundraising dominance—over $2 million combined versus McDonough's $48,000—bolsters the edge ahead of the November general election.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Somaliland's House of Elders (Guurti) approved a 27-month extension of House of Representatives and local council terms on April 28, 2026, citing security concerns, drought, and National Electoral Commission (NEC) technical challenges, postponing parliamentary elections originally slated for May 2026 into late 2028. This recent decision drives trader consensus at 78.5% for no election before 2027, amid criticism from President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi (Irro) of Waddani Party—fresh off its 2024 presidential victory—over risks to democratic credibility. Waddani leads conditional odds at 29.4% on presidential momentum, while former ruling Kulmiye trails at 1.6% post-defeat, and UCID at 5.2%, reflecting limited prospects absent a snap vote reversal.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Recent April developments, including plans to curb post-IPO selloffs and retail allocation emphasis, keep the race tight ahead of the late-May S-1 filing.

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the best Math AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Math" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/math-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will 80 or more ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between May 11-May 17?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from May 11, 2026, through May 17, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 51% implied probability to fewer than 20 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during the week of May 11, reflecting persistent disruptions from the ongoing 2026 Hormuz crisis amid U.S.-Iran hostilities, including blockades, Iranian attacks, and heightened insurance risks that have slashed traffic from pre-crisis norms of over 80 vessels daily to a trickle of 9-21 crossings on May 1-3. The 29.5% odds on 20-39 ships capture potential escorted transits, with higher bins trailing on sustained risk aversion; watch for U.S. Navy updates and diplomacy as resolution nears.

Fragmented opposition—including Nikolai Denkov of the centrist We Continue the Change-Democratic Bulgaria bloc at 11.5%, Sofia Mayor Vassil Terziev at 8.8%, and GERB's Rosen Zhelyazkov at 4.5%—struggles post-electoral losses, with no recent polls shifting sentiment. markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Valencia: Pablo Carreno Busta vs Francisco Comesana
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Pablo Carreno Busta and Francisco Comesana in the Valencia, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Pablo Carreno Busta' if Pablo Carreno Busta advances against Francisco Comesana.

This market will resolve to 'Francisco Comesana' if Francisco Comesana advances against Pablo Carreno Busta.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Cavaliers holding a 2-1 series lead over the Pistons in the Eastern Conference Semifinals after a gritty 116-109 Game 3 win on May 9—fueled by Donovan Mitchell's 35 points and James Harden's clutch play—traders heavily favor Over 5.5 total games at 73.5% implied probability, reflecting the series' back-and-forth intensity with no blowouts. Close contests, including Detroit's 107-97 Game 2 upset on the road, underscore matchup parity, bolstered by Cade Cunningham's playoff-leading scoring (302 points) and both teams' robust frontcourts featuring Evan Mobley, Jarrett Allen, and Karl-Anthony Towns. Minor injury concerns—Pistons' Kevin Huerter and Cavaliers' Sam Merrill questionable—haven't derailed stars, while Detroit's home-court edge in Game 4 today and first-round comeback resilience signal potential for six or seven games amid high-stakes playoff momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Vancouver Liberals' Kareem Allam at 38.5% over incumbent Mayor Ken Sim's 32.5% in the October 17 civic election, reflecting a tight first-past-the-post race amid persistent voter frustration with city governance. Sim bolsters his re-election bid with a recent zero property tax increase and incumbency advantages from his 2022 plurality win, while Allam—Sim's former chief of staff—gains traction positioning as a pragmatic alternative appealing to change-seeking moderates. markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: United Russia's commanding 96% trader consensus to secure the most seats in Russia's September 18–20, 2026, State Duma elections stems from its entrenched dominance in the parallel voting system—225 proportional representation seats above a 5% threshold and 225 single-member districts—bolstered by administrative resources, state media control, and regional governor ties. Recent April polls (e.g., FOM: United Russia 37–39%, LDPR and KPRF ~9–10%) show stable leads amid high undecided rates (15–20%), with opposition parties like New People, LDPR, and A Just Russia weakened by internal crises, legal pressures, and Kremlin oversight, as detailed in preparations reported early May. Kremlin tactics including expanded electronic voting and limited party participation reinforce this positioning. Scenarios challenging this include mass protests over economic woes, elite defections, or war setbacks disrupting turnout, though structural barriers make upsets improbable.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Lee Zeldin leads alternatives at 13% following reports positioning the EPA administrator as Trump's top choice, bolstered by his evasion of AG candidacy questions in recent interviews. Blanche follows at 10.5% on hints of permanent nomination and his aggressive interim actions at DOJ, while Harmeet Dhillon and Ken Paxton trail due to lower visibility in successor speculation despite their Trump-aligned records. Senate confirmation hurdles, including potential holds from swing votes like Sen. Tillis, underscore the contested path.

events.title: Cordoba: Juan Ignacio Gallego vs Ryan Dickerson
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Juan Ignacio Gallego and Ryan Dickerson in the Cordoba, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Juan Ignacio Gallego' if Juan Ignacio Gallego advances against Ryan Dickerson.

This market will resolve to 'Ryan Dickerson' if Ryan Dickerson advances against Juan Ignacio Gallego.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ryan Dickerson, ranked around No. 629 with a career-high of 619, holds a clear experience edge over 19-year-old Argentine wild card Juan Ignacio Gallego (No. 1608, career-high 1598) in this ATP Challenger Cordoba first-round clash on clay. The pair meet for the first time with no head-to-head history, as Gallego earned a main-draw wild card announced days ago, leveraging home-crowd support and familiarity on the slower surface. Dickerson arrives off recent qualifier wins in late April, showing solid form, while Gallego's lower-level results highlight his upside but limited Challenger exposure. No reported injuries or withdrawals; match conditions including potential weather could factor in on game day.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's landslide victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, securing over 70% of delegate support and the official endorsement, has solidified his position as the trader-favored nominee in the September 1 GOP gubernatorial primary, reflecting party establishment momentum and his self-funding as a former biotech executive. markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on a 73.5% implied probability for "No" reflects persistent congressional gridlock in the 119th Congress, where over 100 AI-related bills have been introduced since 2025 but none incorporate the market's required safety provisions—such as prohibitions on specific AI models, training data or parameter limits, broad usage restrictions, or mandatory human-in-the-loop oversight—have reached the floor for votes. The Trump administration's March 2026 National Policy Framework prioritized innovation, federal preemption of state laws, and light-touch standards over stringent safety measures, exacerbating partisan divides with Democrats favoring robust guardrails and Republicans emphasizing competitiveness. Recent bipartisan advances, like the Senate Judiciary Committee's April 30 unanimous approval of a bill curbing AI chatbots for minors, remain narrow and stalled short of full passage. With November 2026 midterms looming and states like Connecticut enacting SB5 on May 1, traders anticipate a federal patchwork persisting through the lame-duck session.

markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, where Freedom Movement (GS) secured 29 seats and Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28 in a fragmented 90-seat National Assembly, coalition negotiations have stalled after incumbent PM Robert Golob conceded failure on April 20 to form a majority. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined a prime minister-designate on April 25, launching a second round of consultations as of early May, with nominations due by May 19 requiring 46 votes for investiture. Janez Janša's SDS is central, pursuing alliance with New Slovenia (NSi)–SLS–Fokus (9 seats), Democrats (6 seats), and Resni.ca (5 seats), who elected Speaker Zoran Stevanović on April 10 and share programmatic overlaps, though Resni.ca prefers external support. Failure risks snap elections.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be 64°F or higher on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The National Weather Service's latest forecast guidance for Chicago projects a high temperature of 65–70°F on May 12, aligning with near-unanimous trader consensus at 99.4% implied probability for 64°F or higher, driven by a building ridge of high pressure over the Great Lakes that ushers in warmer southerly flow following recent cool Canadian air advection. Ensemble model runs from GFS and ECMWF show strong agreement on mostly sunny skies, light winds, and efficient daytime heating under seasonable May normals around 69°F. Over the past 24 hours, refined short-range guidance has solidified this outlook with minimal discrepancies. Realistic challenges include an unanticipated surge of lake-effect clouds or a stalled frontal boundary capping highs below 64°F, though current upper-air patterns make this unlikely ahead of tomorrow's 5 PM CDT resolution window.

markets.question: Will Abdul El-Sayed win the 2026 Michigan Democratic Primary?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Michigan's open Democratic U.S. Senate primary to replace retiring Sen. Gary Peters, trader consensus gives Abdul El-Sayed a 51.5% implied probability of winning, ahead of Mallory McMorrow (28%) and Haley Stevens (19.3%), driven by his polling strength among voters under 40 and Black Democrats, as shown in mid-April Emerson crosstabs where he tied McMorrow at 24%. Recent Glengariff (late April) had Stevens edging El-Sayed 25%-23%, but markets reflect El-Sayed's fundraising surge and May 3 rally with Sen. Bernie Sanders boosting progressive turnout. The race has intensified with mutual attacks and union courtship—UAW yet to endorse ahead of the August 4 primary—highlighting generational and ideological divides in this battleground contest.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?
markets.description:
If Xi Jinping visits the United States between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Xi Jinping physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the United States. Whether or not Xi Jinping enters US airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 72% chance of Chinese President Xi Jinping visiting the United States before 2027, driven by U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated public statements and official announcements signaling a reciprocal state visit later in 2026 following Trump's own trip to Beijing on May 14-15. A White House press briefing in March confirmed plans to host Xi and Madame Peng in Washington, D.C., at a date to be set this year, while intelligence reports and Trump's January comments point to a late-2026 timeline amid stabilizing bilateral diplomacy, including February phone talks and Paris trade negotiations. Ongoing tensions over Taiwan, trade, and the Iran conflict introduce uncertainty, but recent Chinese Foreign Ministry affirmations of Trump's visit bolster expectations for high-level summits, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in pricing diplomatic momentum.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on 2026 inflation peaks reflects recent acceleration in U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI), with March 2026 reading at 3.3% year-over-year—up sharply from 2.4% in February—driven by 12.5% energy price surges and persistent shelter costs, amid tariff passthrough and geopolitical risks. Markets price in further upside, with April CPI due May 12 expected near 3.7%, the highest since 2023. Federal Reserve's March projections quietly raised 2026 PCE inflation forecasts toward 2.6%, while 5-year Treasury breakeven rates hover at 2.61%, signaling moderate long-term expectations versus near-term volatility. Key catalysts include sequential monthly CPI releases through year-end and June FOMC deliberations on policy stance.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - May 12, 11:55AM-12:00PM ET
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's administration has escalated pressure on NATO allies with a May 2 order to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany—beyond an initial January pullback of 200 officers from alliance structures—citing insufficient European burden-sharing and defense spending shortfalls. These moves, echoing first-term threats, aim to compel higher contributions rather than signal full withdrawal, which requires one-year notice under Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty and faces steep congressional and institutional barriers. Trader consensus prices low odds on exit, prioritizing rhetoric's leverage value amid historical non-action. Europe accelerates fallback plans for self-reliance, while upcoming White House talks with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte may address troop commitments and alliance cohesion before any resolution deadlines.

markets.question: Spread: Mohun Bagan Super Giant (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Mohun Bagan Super Giant" if Mohun Bagan Super Giant win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Inter Kashi FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on indiansuperleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Mohun Bagan Super Giant vs. Inter Kashi FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mohun Bagan Super Giant, sitting second in the ISL 2025-26 table with 13 points from six matches including a +11 goal difference, hold strong home form at Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan ahead of their rescheduled clash against mid-table Inter Kashi FC on May 12. The Mariners recently drew 0-0 at Bengaluru FC and sit just behind leaders Mumbai City FC, bolstered by attacking depth and midfield control driving their League Shield push. Inter Kashi, with five points from 1-2-3, struggle away, coming off a 1-2 home loss to Mumbai City and facing potential absences like Mario Barco's injury. Playoff implications loom large for the top six, with Mohun Bagan's superior head-to-head edge and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on a competitive yet lopsided matchup.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

events.title: NJ-11 Special Election: Margin of Victory
events.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Analilia Mejia's commanding 96.9% implied probability for a 20-25% margin of victory in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election stems from post-April 16 vote tallies, where the progressive Democrat leads Republican Joe Hathaway by nearly 20 points with over 95% reporting, outperforming 2024 presidential and Gov. Mikie Sherrill's recent benchmarks by 10-13 points amid high Democratic turnout in this blue-leaning suburban seat. Pre-election polls forecasted her dominance after a competitive Democratic primary win, bolstered by endorsements from labor groups and focus on affordability issues. While certification remains pending, late provisional or absentee ballots from GOP-leaning areas like Livingston and Millburn—where Mejia underperformed due to views on Israel—could theoretically narrow the gap below 20%, though current trends make this improbable.

markets.question: Will Nicolae Ciucă be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Romania's pro-EU coalition government under Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan collapsed on May 5 following a no-confidence vote, driven by the Social Democratic Party (PSD)—the largest parliamentary bloc—withdrawing support amid backlash against austerity measures, temporarily aligning with far-right AUR opposition. This has thrust President Klaus Iohannis into consultations with party leaders to nominate a new premier, with PSD's Sorin Grindeanu leading trader consensus at 24.5% implied probability due to his party's size and prior PM experience, closely trailed by PNL's Cătălin Predoiu at 16.5% as a potential continuity pick. The race remains tight amid fragile coalition talks, possibly involving UDMR or renewed PSD-PNL ties, with snap elections looming if no majority forms; presidential endorsement or key endorsements could swiftly separate frontrunners.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 11, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC)

events.title: Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for Monday, May 11, 2026 between Millwall FC and Hull City AFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following a goalless 0-0 draw in the first leg of their EFL Championship play-off semi-final, trader consensus prices a tight second leg at The Den with Draw (44.5%) edging Millwall FC (38.5%) ahead of Hull City AFC (17.5%), reflecting the high stakes and defensive caution expected in a promotion decider tied on aggregate. Millwall hold home advantage and boast the division's best home clean-sheet record, unbeaten in six outings with strong recent form, but face doubts over Josh Coburn's hip fitness and absences like Massimo Luongo and Lukas Jensen. Hull, winless in five away games, rely on Oli McBurnie's 17 goals and playoff pedigree from two prior promotions, yet contend with injuries to Cody Drameh and Eliot Matazo, fostering a closely contested dynamic primed for low-scoring tension or penalties.

markets.question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from early May, including Futura and Ideia, show President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva holding a slim first-round lead over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro at around 38-40% to 36-37%, with trader consensus on Polymarket reflecting this razor-thin contest at 41.3% for Flávio and 39.5% for Lula amid narrowing gaps. Lula's eroding support stems from rising disapproval ratings above 50%—fueled by cost-of-living squeezes, household debt, and inflation concerns—while Flávio consolidates the right-wing vote through his father's endorsement and the enduring Bolsonaro brand in a polarized electorate. The fragmented field, with no other candidate exceeding 6%, and high undecideds keep the race tight; separation could arise from economic data releases, major endorsements, candidate withdrawals, or scandals ahead of the October 4 first-round vote, potentially forcing a runoff.

This three-part true crime docuseries, which premiered April 29, has fueled massive binge-watching through its gripping real-life tale of forensic pathologist Caroline Muirhead's toxic romance, surging via strong word-of-mouth and social buzz in the competitive May slate. Challengers like "Legends" (2%) or "Unchosen" linger with modest momentum, but an upset would require a dramatic late-week viewership spike from new episodes or viral breaks before charts lock, though historical patterns show established leaders rarely slip in final tallies.

Recent coalition talks collapsed last week after Moderates leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen, holding kingmaker leverage with 14 seats, blocked red alliances and nominated Venstre's Troels Lund Poulsen as formateur on May 8; Poulsen's odds surged to 17% as he launches cross-party negotiations today. Centrist dynamics and historical precedents in hung parliaments underscore uncertainty ahead of potential government formation or prolonged deadlock.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 44°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD)'s latest forecast, issued May 10, projects Lucknow's May 12 maximum temperature at 38°C with clear skies and minimums around 26°C, driving the market's 92.5% implied probability for 38°C or below as traders align with this authoritative guidance. Recent observations confirm suppressed highs—35°C on May 10 (-4.7°C below normal) and 33.9°C on May 9—stemming from lingering effects of unseasonal rainfall and thunderstorms earlier in May 2026, which have cooled the region below typical pre-monsoon averages near 40°C. This skin-in-the-game consensus acknowledges low forecast uncertainty for one-day horizons, though scenarios like intensified solar heating under prolonged clear conditions or upper-air warming could realistically nudge it to 39°C; watch IMD's daily update on May 11 for refinements.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With one year until France's 2027 presidential election—first round expected in April—traders weigh a fragmented field where Rassemblement National candidates like Jordan Bardella lead first-round polls at around 30-35% per recent Toluna Harris Interactive and RTL surveys, reflecting persistent voter discontent post-2024 hung parliament and municipal results. Jean-Luc Mélenchon officially launched his fourth bid for La France Insoumise on May 3 amid left-wing primary talks, while Édouard Philippe kicked off his centre-right Horizons campaign over the weekend, polling best against RN in runoffs. Les Républicains designated Bruno Retailleau; others like Raphaël Glucksmann ponder runs. Ballot access requires 500 signatures from elected officials across departments, with primaries and declarations likely to shape the field ahead of potential second-round clash.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in custody following a February 2026 Seoul Central District Court life sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his December 2024 martial law declaration, upheld amid ongoing appeals. An April 29 appeals court ruling increased his separate seven-year term for obstructing arrest and bypassing cabinet processes, extending detention amid multiple charges including abuse of power. With trials continuing into mid-2026 and no bail grants or procedural releases since early detention extensions, trader consensus at 92.2% for "No" reflects judicial momentum toward prolonged incarceration, barring successful higher-court reversals or procedural breaks before 2027.

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner at 18.6%, bolstered by his recent Iowa caucus-state visit where he drew warm Republican support, reinforcing his positioning as the GOP frontrunner despite some polling softening. California Governor Gavin Newsom follows closely at 16.7%, maintaining Democratic primary strength shown in March California surveys trouncing Kamala Harris. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's 13.3% reflects a viral White House briefing clip sparking "Marcomentum" and positioning him as a Vance rival. This tight contest highlights open-seat uncertainty after President Trump's term limits, with 2026 midterms, early polls, and primary announcements likely to create separation in battleground states.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: ONIC PH vs Omega Esports (BO3) - MPL Philippines Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between ONIC PH and Omega Esports in the MPL Philippines Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 15 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ONIC PH" if ONIC PH win the match against Omega Esports.

This market will resolve to "Omega Esports" if Omega Esports win the match against ONIC PH.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: ONIC Philippines enters their Week 8 BO3 rematch against Omega Esports on May 15 with momentum from a 2-1 grind over Aurora Gaming PH last Sunday, bolstering their third-place standing at 8-4 match record in MPL PH Season 17's double round robin regular season. Omega, sitting fourth at 6-6, showed resilience by upsetting second-place Team Falcons PH 2-1 on May 9 after a 0-2 loss to leaders Team Liquid PH, highlighting their upset potential in playoffs where top-two seeds earn upper bracket byes. ONIC previously swept Omega 2-0 on April 17, with stable rosters featuring Super Frince and K1NGKONG for ONIC, and Raizen for Omega; this clash could shift seeding as the regular season concludes May 17.

markets.question: Will Annie January (Starlight) die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
markets.description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

Showrunner Eric Kripke has teased Survivor-style eliminations building to the May 20 finale, with Homelander's V1 serum injection signaling power shifts. Leaks speculate Frenchie and Soldier Boy casualties, but only aired kills count for resolution; fan calendar theories add buzz without proof. Volatility peaks as Episodes 7-8 loom, rewarding close tracking of weekly drops over unverified spoilers.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 36.3% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the Central Bank's latest REM survey median of 30.5% end-year, up 1.4 percentage points from prior estimates. This shift stems from March 2026 CPI rising 3.4% month-over-month—above consensus—pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9.4% amid seasonal education and transport pressures, despite President Milei's austerity measures curbing annual rates to 32.6% from February's 33.2%. Lower bins like 25-29.9% (24.5%) reflect optimism for further fiscal tightening, while upside risks above 40% (24.2% combined) account for potential policy slippage; April CPI data, due imminently, looms as the next catalyst.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30, driven by the absence of any scheduled summit amid fragile Ukraine ceasefire talks initiated via their April 29 phone discussion, which led to a short-lived three-day pause marred by mutual violation accusations. Putin's May 9 statement signaling the Ukraine conflict is "coming to an end" and openness to meeting Zelenskyy in a third country has not prompted Trump-Putin logistics, as the White House confirmed no immediate plans. Trump's focus on the May 14-15 summit with China's Xi Jinping, overshadowed by Iran war dynamics, further delays bilateral diplomacy, with a potential G20 encounter in Miami deferred to December. Low odds on venues like China reflect Kremlin denials of joint appearances there.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid the fragile ceasefire in the 2026 Iran war, extended by President Trump in late April and mediated by Pakistan, U.S.-Iran indirect negotiations advanced with Iran's response to Washington's latest one-page peace proposal delivered via Pakistani intermediaries on May 10. Prior Islamabad talks featured U.S. delegations including VP JD Vance, Jared Kushner, and envoy Steve Witkoff opposite Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, though sessions stalled after 21 hours. Recent U.S. review of Iran's counterproposal sustains trader focus on attendance for the next round, potentially next week, amid Strait of Hormuz tensions and oil market volatility. Official announcements on participants remain pending.

markets.question: Will 20-39 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between May 11-May 17?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from May 11, 2026, through May 17, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 51% implied probability to fewer than 20 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during the week of May 11, reflecting persistent disruptions from the ongoing 2026 Hormuz crisis amid U.S.-Iran hostilities, including blockades, Iranian attacks, and heightened insurance risks that have slashed traffic from pre-crisis norms of over 80 vessels daily to a trickle of 9-21 crossings on May 1-3. Recent stealth exits of two crude oil tankers with transponders off underscore cautious flows, while President Trump's May 11 rejection of Iran's peace counteroffer prolongs uncertainty, pricing in low volumes absent de-escalation. The 29.5% odds on 20-39 ships capture potential escorted transits, with higher bins trailing on sustained risk aversion; watch for U.S. Navy updates and diplomacy as resolution nears.

markets.question: Darderi vs. Zverev: Match O/U 23.5

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Luciano Darderi vs Alexander Zverev
events.description:

This market will resolve to 'Luciano Darderi' if Luciano Darderi advances against Alexander Zverev.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Zverev' if Alexander Zverev advances against Luciano Darderi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Second-seeded Alexander Zverev (world No. 3), fresh off straight-sets wins over Daniel Altmaier and Alexander Blockx to reach the Internazionali BNL d'Italia round of 16, holds a 1-0 head-to-head edge over Luciano Darderi from their 2024 clash here on clay. markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just one month away starting June 11, trader consensus on nations reaching the final hinges on the December 2025 group draw and paths through the expanded 24-team knockout from the round of 32, favoring Spain (Group H with Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Uruguay), France (Group I vs. Senegal, Iraq, Norway), and England (Group L vs. Croatia, Ghana, Panama) due to manageable group stages and opposite-bracket semifinal routes against Argentina or Brazil. Recent UEFA playoff triumphs in late March propelled Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye as potential dark horses, while mounting injuries—Rodrygo's ACL tear sidelining Brazil's depth, Germany's Serge Gnabry out with a torn adductor, and Spain's Lamine Yamal hamstring scare—introduce uncertainty amid final squad announcements. Host advantages boost USA (Group D), Mexico (Group A opener), and Canada (Group B), but no major developments have shifted standings in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).
