If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Korea Meteorological Administration, specifically the figure for monthly precipitation in mm when the classification is set for "ground", the region/branch is set for "Seoul", the element is set for "precipitation", and the period is set for the month of May at the https://data.kma.go.kr/climate/RankState/selectRankStatisticsDivisionList.do link once the figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Spread: Randers FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for May 11 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Randers FC" if Randers FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Odense BK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on superligaen.dk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Randers FC vs. Odense BK - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for May 11 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Danish Superliga relegation round, Odense BK (2nd, 34 points, GD -10) host Randers FC (3rd, 30 points, GD -6) on April 19 at Nature Energy Park, a pivotal matchup with just four points separating the sides amid the battle to avoid playoffs. Both teams stumbled in their April 12 fixtures—Odense fell 3-1 at Silkeborg IF after a strong 1-0 home win over FC Fredericia on April 5, while Randers lost 2-1 to FC Copenhagen following a 1-1 draw at Vejle BK. Odense boasts solid home form and a slight head-to-head edge, including a 3-2 victory in the August 2025 reverse fixture, but Randers' Oliver Jones remains out with a knee injury. Recent low-scoring trends and travel for Randers could influence trader consensus on a tight contest.

markets.description:

The resolution source for this market is Ornnai.com (ornnai.com), specifically, the H200 Index chart data available at https://dashboard.ornnai.com. The daily values shown on the chart will be used for resolution. Daily data will be considered finalized once the following day’s data point is published.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed price is hit, or once the value for the specified end date is finalized. If not all relevant data has been finalized by the end of the 14th calendar date afterward (ET), this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to previously published data points, before all relevant data points have been finalized, will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after all relevant data points have been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment prices H200 GPU rental rates amid surging AI compute demand outpacing NVIDIA supply, with spot/on-demand listings on Vast.ai and Runpod at $2.70-$4.00 per GPU-hour in early May 2026—up 20-40% year-to-date per SemiAnalysis indexes—while neoclouds like CoreWeave charge $6+/hr premiums. Persistent shortages, fueled by large model training and inference workloads, have driven recent spikes, as evidenced by Silicon Data tracking H100s at $2.75/hr equivalents. NVIDIA's Blackwell ramp (B200 at $5+/hr) adds downward pressure potential, but hyperscaler contracts sustain tightness. Key watch: supply announcements or utilization data before May 31 resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton holds a trader consensus edge at 59.5% implied probability over incumbent Senator John Cornyn's 40% ahead of the May 26 GOP Senate primary runoff, reflecting Paxton's sustained lead in recent polls like the University of Houston Hobby School survey showing him at 48% to Cornyn's 45% among likely Republican voters. Paxton's strong appeal to the GOP base, bolstered by his Trump alignment and criticisms of Cornyn's past support for bipartisan gun control measures like the Safer Communities Act, has driven momentum despite Cornyn's fundraising advantage and establishment backing. Trump's reluctance to endorse keeps the race tight, with high base turnout and early voting underway as key factors that could tip the balance in this closely contested matchup.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' closely contested implied probabilities—clustered tightly around the 2.1%–2.9% bins for South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation—reflect April 2026's year-over-year headline rate accelerating to 2.6%, a 21-month high driven by surging petroleum prices amid Middle East tensions, while core inflation held steady at 2.2%. This uptick from March's 2.2% has edged sentiment toward the higher 2.7%–2.9% outcome at 33.3%, though Bank of Korea projections of mid-to-high 2% averages, IMF's 2.5% forecast, and stable underlying pressures keep lower bins competitive at 29.9%–32.1%. Key swing factors include May CPI data and the BOK's late-May policy meeting, where persistent energy costs could prompt a hawkish pivot from the 2.50% base rate.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 American Rodeo semifinals roster finalized after the West Regional Finals concluded May 2 in Las Vegas, trader consensus centers on top PRCA-ranked invitees and regional standouts in bareback riding, where elite spur riders like Keenan Hayes lead sentiment due to consistent 85+ point rides on tough broncs this season and prior podium finishes at Globe Life Field. East Regional winner Tilden Hooper, Central qualifiers Ty Pope and Leighton Berry, plus West advancers including Richmond Champion and Chett Deitz, earned spots via aggregate scores, bolstering a competitive 20-rider field for the May 22 semifinals. Recent PRCA momentum favors high-output veterans like Rocker Steiner and Wacey Schalla, while random draws and bucking horse matchups loom large ahead of the May 23 championship round for the $1 million bonus.

markets.question: Will Laurent Blanc be appointed as manager of Manchester United?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Manchester United.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Manchester United; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Michael Carrick leads trader consensus at 85% implied probability as Manchester United's next permanent manager, driven by his successful interim stint since Ruben Amorim's January sacking amid a dismal Premier League campaign.** Recent reports confirm full board backing, with key decision-makers discarding rivals like Thomas Tuchel (4%) and Kieran McKenna (3.4%), prioritizing Carrick's seamless player relationships, fan support, and unbeaten record against top-six sides including upcoming Liverpool clash. Pundit endorsements, such as Jamie Carragher's call for stability at Old Trafford post-multiple managerial upheavals, have surged his odds, while Tuchel's experience lingers as a fallback despite lacking club-specific momentum. End-of-season Champions League qualification pushes could seal his appointment.

markets.question: Will CZ post 20-39 posts from May 8 to May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between May 8, 12:00 PM ET and May 15, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices CZ posting fewer than 20 times on X from May 8–15 at 79%, driven by his sparse activity in the period's opening days—just three qualifying posts (originals and quotes, excluding replies) on May 8–9, none since amid apparent post-travel recovery from Consensus 2026 appearances in Miami on May 7. The prior May 1–8 market resolved to 25–30 posts, establishing a 3–4 daily baseline, but current slowdown positions the under-20 outcome as frontrunner, with 20–39 at 20% reflecting historical norms absent new catalysts like book events or crypto market volatility. Remaining days hold uncertainty, though no scheduled interviews or announcements signal a posting surge.

markets.question: Will the Starship for SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 achieve a successful splashdown?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Starship upper stage for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight achieves a controlled splashdown in the ocean. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market pertains to the twelfth Starship launch. If the rocket lifts off the pad, it will be considered the twelfth Starship launch, and this market will resolve based on it. If the rocket does not lift off the pad, it will not be considered the twelfth launch, and this market will stay open until the twelfth launch has occurred. If the twelfth launch has not occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

A splashdown will be considered controlled if the Starship upper stage successfully re-enters Earth's atmosphere, remains intact during descent, and enters the water without breaking apart or exploding upon impact.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's Starship Flight Test 12 nears liftoff with a successful launch rehearsal on May 11, 2026, marking the first propellant loading of over 5,000 metric tons on the fully stacked Super Heavy V3 booster and Starship V3 upper stage at Starbase's Pad 2. This follows a full-thrust 33-engine static fire on May 7 and the inaugural V3 stack on May 9, demonstrating rapid progress in reusability milestones for Booster 19 and Ship 39. Trader sentiment reflects growing confidence in hitting the NET May 15 window per marine notices, though FAA licensing, weather, and final checks remain key risks amid SpaceX's iterative test cadence.

markets.question: Will the 2026 trade deficit be between 900B and 1T?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices the 2026 US trade deficit tightly between $800–900 billion (33%) and $900 billion–$1 trillion (32.5%), driven by volatile early-year data and uncertain tariff effects under President Trump's agenda. The Bureau of Economic Analysis reported a March goods and services deficit widening to $60.3 billion—up 4.4% from February—fueled by a 2.3% import surge outpacing 2% export growth, with the 12-month total through February at $776 billion. Despite record 2025 goods shortfalls near $1.2 trillion and higher import duties, strong consumer spending and dollar strength sustain high volumes, offsetting policy impacts. Year-to-date Q1 averages suggest sub-$700 billion annualized paces but historical seasonality favors larger later deficits; separation could arise from April–May BEA releases, further tariff escalations on China or allies, Federal Reserve rate decisions altering currency dynamics, or GDP slowdowns curbing imports.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have a #1 AI model by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If a listed model ties for #1 Arena rank, it will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes."

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the race for the top AI model by December 31 hinges on the intense competition among closed-source frontrunners OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and Anthropic, whose large language models dominate benchmarks like the LMSYS Chatbot Arena and Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Instant, released May 5, solidified its edge with 52.5% fewer hallucinations and superior reasoning, briefly reclaiming the lead from Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview and Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7. Chinese open-weight challengers like Moonshot's Kimi K2.6 and DeepSeek V4 Pro are narrowing the gap to within 6 points on intelligence metrics but trail on agentic coding and hard reasoning tasks. Recent U.S. government pre-release review pacts with Google, xAI, and others signal heightened regulatory scrutiny, while anticipated frontier model drops from all major labs before year-end could swing leaderboard positions amid rapid iteration cycles.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 12 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Bilibili Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 12 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Invictus Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming tops LPL Group Ascend standings at 9-2 with an 18-7 map differential, fresh off a 2-0 sweep of JD Gaming, fueling trader consensus on their favoritism against mid-table Invictus Gaming (5-7, 13-16 maps). IG gained momentum with recent 2-0 wins over Ninjas in Pyjamas and Team WE, but their poor 20% win rate over the past month and 1-2 loss to BLG three weeks ago underscore vulnerabilities. BLG holds a commanding head-to-head edge, unbeaten against IG since August 2025 across five BO3s, with Knight's midlane dominance a key factor in this double round-robin clash critical for playoff seeding. No roster changes or injury concerns reported ahead of the May 12 BO3.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Dallas be between 88-89°F on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Dallas Love Field Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Dallas Love Field Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/dallas/KDAL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in National Weather Service forecasts for Dallas's May 12 high temperature, with models like GFS and ECMWF clustering around 82-85°F following a slow-moving cold front that triggered severe thunderstorms and showers over the May 10-11 weekend. Residual cloud cover and mid-level moisture from the front cap peak heating, favoring 82-83°F (30.5% implied probability) over slightly warmer 84-85°F (24.5%), differentiated by afternoon clearing rates and boundary-layer mixing efficiency. Climatological May 12 averages hover near 83°F, but post-frontal cooling adds variability; watch evening updates from NOAA for refined guidance as resolution nears.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors a highest temperature of 17°C at 66.5% implied probability in Buenos Aires today, closely tracking the latest Servicio Meteorológico Nacional (SMN) short-range forecast projecting a maximum near 17°C under stable conditions with moderate northerly winds and partial cloud cover. This positioning stems from a persistent southerly airflow over the past 48 hours, which has cooled the region after early May highs averaging 20°C, aligning with ensemble guidance from GFS and ECMWF models showing limited diurnal warming potential. Historical May data indicate typical highs of 18–19°C at Aeroparque Jorge Newbery station, but current polar air mass intrusion has shifted expectations lower; updated SMN observations this afternoon could refine probabilities if insolation exceeds projections.
